I reviewed the federal Form 1065, partner K-1 allocations, and the M-1 reconciliation for Northstar Legal FY2025. The support is sufficient and the positions are well supported.

Please treat this reply as my authorization of record for the closed-period late-post to northstar_legal FP-2025-12. I approve booking the $4,820.30 adjustment as DR 530000 SALT expense and CR 230000 accrued SALT payable.

BROOKFIELD CPAs & ADVISORS
COMPLIANCE / AML CASE FILE — CONFIDENTIAL (RESTRICTED)

Case reference:  AML threshold alert on JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052
Client / Entity: Acme Cloud (acme_cloud)
Prepared by:     Marina Soko, Compliance Officer
Date:            2026-06-12
Status:          CLOSED — CLEARED, NO SAR

1. TRIGGER
The firm's standing FinCEN wire-monitoring rule ($10,000 threshold) fired on the
2026-04-22 posting to account 101000 Cash - Operating in period acme_cloud_FP-2026-04.
Flagged item: JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052 (Oracle GL id je_b2c2b939a1244823), a
settlement receipt from Acme Cloud's largest enterprise SaaS customer.

2. LEDGER VERIFICATION
JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052, posted 2026-04-22, source module AR, standard balanced entry:
   DR 101000 Cash - Operating              $57,077.69
   CR 110000 Accounts Receivable - Trade   $57,077.69
The cash posting clears a trade receivable rather than recording an unexplained inflow,
consistent with collection of contracted SaaS revenue. The amount ties to the wire
confirmation reference and to the JE total. Prepared by Owen Mercer; approved and posted
by Edith Banda. Cash posting lines are consistent with the documented CDD rationale.

3. ANALYST REVIEW (CDD) — Farah Dlamini, AML Analyst
   - Counterparty aligns to the beneficial-owner file; jurisdiction and UBO chain match the
     onboarding documentation (ref: Acme Cloud FY2026 Beneficial Owner Refresh;
     Acme Cloud FY2026 AML Risk Assessment Memo).
   - Source of funds: contracted SaaS revenue received on the documented schedule.
   - Wire confirmation reference aligns to the JE amount and standing payment instructions.
   - Recommendation: CLEAR, no SAR.

4. DISPOSITION & APPROVALS
   - Supervisory gate:  Anita Knowles (AML Supervisory Officer) — approved.
   - Partner approval:  Steven Perry (Managing Partner) — approved.
   - Outcome: CLEARED, no SAR filed. No further monitoring action required for this alert.
   - Standing control: FY2027 Beneficial Owner refresh + AML risk re-assessment continues
     on the existing annual cadence.

5. RETENTION & CLASSIFICATION
   Classification: Restricted.
   Retention:      AICPA_SQMS_7Y.
   Basis:          Anchored to the firm's existing Acme Cloud AML precedent — FY2026 AML
                   Risk Assessment Memo and FY2026 Beneficial Owner Refresh (both Restricted,
                   AICPA_SQMS_7Y).

Compliance housekeeping for this case is complete as of 2026-06-12: ledger verified,
CDD disposition memorialized, AML threshold-alert tickler closed.
EOF
base64 -w0 /tmp/cdd_memo.txt > /tmp/cdd_memo.b64

input.content:
Hi Matthew, Steven,

Closing the loop on the Acme Cloud AML wire-monitoring alert so you can correlate it against the original alert. The flagged item was JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052 (Oracle GL id je_b2c2b939a1244823) — the settlement receipt from Acme's largest enterprise SaaS customer that tripped the standing FinCEN $10,000 wire-monitoring threshold on the 2026-04-22 posting to account 101000 Cash - Operating.

Ledger verification: the JE is a balanced AR receipt — DR 101000 Cash - Operating $57,077.69 / CR 110000 Accounts Receivable - Trade $57,077.69. The cash posting clears a trade receivable, consistent with the documented CDD rationale (source of funds = contracted SaaS revenue; amount ties to the wire confirmation reference). No inconsistency.

Disposition: CLEARED, no SAR — Farah Dlamini ran the counterparty/BO/source-of-funds analyst pass; Anita Knowles provided supervisory approval and Steven partner approval.

Compliance housekeeping now fully closed on our side:
- CDD Clearance & Disposition Memo filed to Records Vault (doc_8225c817a4c0), classified Restricted with AICPA_SQMS_7Y retention, linked to the JE and anchored to the firm's existing Acme Cloud AML precedent (FY2026 AML Risk Assessment Memo + Beneficial Owner Refresh).
- Stale AML threshold-alert reminder dismissed.
- FY2027 BO refresh + AML re-assessment remains on the standing annual control.

The file is fully closed on the compliance side ahead of the next review cycle.

Best,
Marina

Marina Soko
Compliance Officer, Brookfield CPAs & Advisors
